Package org.rodinp.core.indexer

Interface IIndexQuery

All Known Implementing Classes:
IndexQuery
public interface IIndexQuery
Common protocol for performing queries to the indexing system.

Queries return either declarations (IDeclaration) or occurrences ( IOccurrence). Basic queries consist of the following:

  • getDeclaration(IInternalElement)
  • getDeclarations(IRodinFile)
  • getVisibleDeclarations(IRodinFile)
  • getDeclarations(IRodinProject, String)
  • getOccurrences(IDeclaration)
  • getOccurrences(IDeclaration, IPropagator)

A basic query result might be too coarse. Queries provide filters on declarations and occurrences to get a more precise result.

Declarations can be filtered out with:

  • a name
  • an element type
Occurrences can be filtered out with:
  • a file
  • an occurrence kind
  • a containing location
Further filtering is left to clients.

As an example of combining basic queries and filters, we may write the following code: 

 IIndexQuery query = RodinCore.makeIndexQuery();
 query.waitUpToDate();
 
 IDeclaration declMySet = query.getDeclaration(mySet);
 if (declMySet != null) { // the element is indexed
        Set<IOccurrence> occsMySet = query.getOccurrences(declMySet);
        query.filterFile(occsMySet, myFile);
        // process occurrences of mySet in myFile
 }

Query results may be obsolete if the indexing system has performed a new indexing since they were computed. Clients should call waitUpToDate() before performing queries, as illustrated in the above example, in order to get the most accurate result.

A query can be obtained through RodinCore.makeIndexQuery().

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    filterFile(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IRodinFile file)
    Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones located in the given file.
    void
    filterKind(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IOccurrenceKind kind)
    Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones of the given kind.
    void
    filterLocation(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IInternalLocation location)
    Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones included in the given location.
    void
    filterName(Set<IDeclaration> declarations, String name)
    Filters out the given declarations, keeping only the ones with the given name.
    void
    filterType(Set<IDeclaration> declarations, IInternalElementType<?> type)
    Filters out the given declarations, keeping only the ones that refer to an element of the given type.
    IDeclaration
    getDeclaration(IInternalElement element)
    Returns the declaration for the given element.
    Set<IDeclaration>
    getDeclarations(Collection<IOccurrence> occurrences)
    Returns the declarations associated with the given occurrences.
    Set<IDeclaration>
    getDeclarations(IRodinFile file)
    Returns the declarations of all indexed elements that occur in the given file.
    Set<IDeclaration>
    getDeclarations(IRodinProject project, String name)
    Returns all elements declared within the given project with the given user-visible name.
    Set<IOccurrence>
    getOccurrences(Collection<IDeclaration> declarations)
    Returns all occurrences of the given declarations.
    Set<IOccurrence>
    getOccurrences(IDeclaration declaration)
    Returns all occurrences of the given declaration.
    Set<IOccurrence>
    getOccurrences(IDeclaration declaration, IPropagator propagator)
    Returns all occurrences of the given declaration and propagates the call on declarations provided by the given propagator.
    Set<IDeclaration>
    getVisibleDeclarations(IRodinFile file)
    Returns the declarations of all indexed elements that are visible in the given file.
    void
    waitUpToDate()
    Blocks until the indexing system is up to date or this thread gets interrupted.
    void
    waitUpToDate(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Blocks until the indexing system is up to date, the given monitor is cancelled or this thread gets interrupted.

  • Method Details

    • waitUpToDate

      void waitUpToDate() throws InterruptedException
      Blocks until the indexing system is up to date or this thread gets interrupted.

      To ensure accurate results, clients should call this method before querying the index.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted before or while it is waiting

    • waitUpToDate

      void waitUpToDate(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws InterruptedException
      Blocks until the indexing system is up to date, the given monitor is cancelled or this thread gets interrupted.

      To ensure accurate results, clients should call this method before querying the index.

      Parameters:
      monitor - a progress monitor that can cancel the wait
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the current thread is interrupted before or while it is waiting
      Since:
      1.6

    • getDeclaration

      IDeclaration getDeclaration(IInternalElement element)
      Returns the declaration for the given element. If the element is not indexed, null is returned.
      Parameters:
      element - the element for which the declaration is desired
      Returns:
      the declaration of the given element or null if not found

    • getDeclarations

      Set<IDeclaration> getDeclarations(IRodinFile file)
      Returns the declarations of all indexed elements that occur in the given file. A returned declaration can belong to the given file. Alternatively, a returned declaration can belong to another file, as soon as it has an associated occurrence in the given file.
      Parameters:
      file - the file to search in
      Returns:
      the declarations that have an occurrence in the given file
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • getVisibleDeclarations

      Set<IDeclaration> getVisibleDeclarations(IRodinFile file)
      Returns the declarations of all indexed elements that are visible in the given file. In other words, it returns getDeclarations(file) plus visible (imported) elements that do not occur in file, but could.
      Parameters:
      file - the file to search in
      Returns:
      the declarations that are visible in the given file
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • getDeclarations

      Set<IDeclaration> getDeclarations(IRodinProject project, String name)
      Returns all elements declared within the given project with the given user-visible name.
      Parameters:
      project - the project to search in
      name - the name to search for
      Returns:
      the declarations found in the given project with the given name

    • getOccurrences

      Set<IOccurrence> getOccurrences(IDeclaration declaration)
      Returns all occurrences of the given declaration.
      Parameters:
      declaration - the declaration for which occurrences are desired
      Returns:
      the indexed occurrences of the given declaration

    • getOccurrences

      Set<IOccurrence> getOccurrences(IDeclaration declaration, IPropagator propagator)
      Returns all occurrences of the given declaration and propagates the call on declarations provided by the given propagator.

      Initially, the propagator is called on each occurrence of the given declaration. If the propagator returns a non null declaration on some of them, all occurrences of these declarations are added to the result, and the propagator is called again on the new occurrences. The process stops when the propagator returns only null values.

      This method is particularly intended to deal with cases where various elements represent the same entity.

      Parameters:
      declaration - the declaration for which occurrences are desired
      propagator - the propagator to propagate the call with
      Returns:
      a set of propagated declarations
      Since:
      0.9.3
      See Also:
      • IPropagator

    • getOccurrences

      Set<IOccurrence> getOccurrences(Collection<IDeclaration> declarations)
      Returns all occurrences of the given declarations.
      Parameters:
      declarations - the declarations for which occurrences are desired
      Returns:
      the indexed occurrences of the given declarations
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • getDeclarations

      Set<IDeclaration> getDeclarations(Collection<IOccurrence> occurrences)
      Returns the declarations associated with the given occurrences.
      Parameters:
      occurrences - the occurrences for which declarations are desired
      Returns:
      the declarations corresponding to the given occurrences
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • filterType

      void filterType(Set<IDeclaration> declarations, IInternalElementType<?> type)
      Filters out the given declarations, keeping only the ones that refer to an element of the given type.
      Parameters:
      declarations - a set of declarations
      type - the type to filter with
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • filterName

      void filterName(Set<IDeclaration> declarations, String name)
      Filters out the given declarations, keeping only the ones with the given name.
      Parameters:
      declarations - a set of declarations
      name - the name to filter with
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • filterKind

      void filterKind(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IOccurrenceKind kind)
      Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones of the given kind.
      Parameters:
      occurrences - a set of occurrences
      kind - the kind to filter with
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • filterFile

      void filterFile(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IRodinFile file)
      Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones located in the given file.
      Parameters:
      occurrences - a set of occurrences
      file - the file to filter with
      Since:
      0.9.3

    • filterLocation

      void filterLocation(Set<IOccurrence> occurrences, IInternalLocation location)
      Filters out the given occurrences, keeping only the ones included in the given location.
      Parameters:
      occurrences - a set of occurrences
      location - the location to filter with
      Since:
      0.9.3
